items.title: The dot-com crash was a $5 trillion blip. Why the next financial crisis could hit 4 times harder.
items.description: The next global financial crisis is already under way. Here is how it will unfold.

items.title: ‘The money would have been life-changing’: My father told my grandmother to slash my inheritance. Is that fair?
items.description: “I could have bought a home for what I hope will be a growing family.”

items.title: Meta’s stock rebounds as agentic AI coding and custom chips ease spending fears
items.description: Shares of Meta rallied on Thursday following the launch of a new AI model and reports of a big custom-chip milestone.

items.title: A slower AI payoff risks tipping the economy into recession, Apollo says
items.description: Growing threats from China and falling token prices may put AI financials at risk.

items.title: Meet the new group of stocks powering the S&P 500 higher
items.description: A violent rotation is taking place under the hood of the stock market ahead of second-quarter earnings.

items.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
items.description: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”

items.title: Landon Donovan says there’s ‘zero chance’ he could have afforded to play youth soccer in America if he was a kid today
items.description: The U.S. men’s soccer team once again didn’t make it past the Round of 16 in the World Cup — and some say the high cost to play youth soccer in America could be a key reason.

items.title: New ‘Ex-Elon’ ETFs let you avoid SpaceX and Tesla — but are they just a gimmick?
items.description: One expert doubts that new ETFs from Subversive will catch on in a meaningful way.

items.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
items.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”
